questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To sell Notion templates directly to users.
options.explanation: The article mentions templates, but selling them is only a part of one lesson, not the Academy’s main purpose.

options.text: To help users learn Notion, earn badges, and become certified admins.
options.explanation: The opening section states that Notion Academy turns curiosity into capability, offers badges, and provides certification for admins.

options.text: To provide a marketplace for third‑party integrations.
options.explanation: Integrations are mentioned in a lesson, but the Academy itself isn’t a marketplace.

options.text: To host live webinars exclusively for enterprise customers.
options.explanation: Live webinars are offered to all users, not just enterprises, and they are just one of many resources.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about earning a Notion badge is correct?

options.text: Badges are automatically granted to anyone who signs up for Notion Academy.
options.explanation: Badges are earned by completing paths or lessons, not just by signing up.

options.text: You earn a badge by completing a curated learning path at your own pace.
options.explanation: The article explains that curated paths help you level up and earn badges after you finish them.

options.text: Badges are only available to users on the Enterprise plan.
options.explanation: Badges are available to any plan type; the article does not restrict them to Enterprise.

options.text: Badges can be purchased directly from the Notion Marketplace.
options.explanation: Badges are earned through learning, not bought.

questions.question: What does the "Notion Certified Admin" credential validate?

options.text: Ability to develop custom Notion plugins using JavaScript.
options.explanation: The certification focuses on workspace administration and security, not plugin development.

options.text: Skills to run Notion at scale, design workspaces, and implement security best practices.
options.explanation: The article states the certification is for building smarter workspaces, security, and being recognized as a Certified Admin.

options.text: Expertise in graphic design for Notion pages.
options.explanation: Graphic design is not mentioned as part of the admin certification.

options.text: Proficiency in selling Notion templates on the marketplace.
options.explanation: Template creation is a separate lesson, unrelated to the admin certification.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn how to automate repetitive work using schedules or triggers, which lesson should they start with?

options.explanation: That lesson focuses on building consistent, connected systems, not automation.
The "Custom Agents" lesson is described as helping automate repeat work with schedules or triggers.
This lesson covers creating and selling templates, not automation.
Project management teaches task databases, not automation agents.

questions.question: Which learning path contains the most lessons?

options.explanation: Essentials Path consists of 3 lessons, fewer than the other paths.
Workflows Path has 4 lessons.
Advanced Path includes 5 lessons, the highest count among the listed paths.
There is no "Custom Agents Path" listed; it is a single lesson.

questions.question: A beginner wants to learn the fundamentals of navigating Notion, using blocks, and building simple databases. Which lesson best matches this goal?

options.explanation: The "Building basics" lesson is described as teaching navigation, blocks, custom databases, and wikis—perfect for beginners.
Workspace design is an advanced lesson focusing on architecture, not basic navigation.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: That lesson is intermediate and focuses on intake systems, not basic building blocks.
Templates & marketplace is about creating and selling templates, not core navigation.

questions.question: Which lesson is categorized as "Intermediate" and focuses on scaling knowledge within Notion?

options.explanation: The lesson "Connecting knowledge in Notion" is labeled Intermediate and aims to scale knowledge through consistent, connected systems.
Sharing & collaboration is a Beginner lesson about teamwork, not scaling knowledge.
Workspace design is an Advanced lesson focused on design best practices.
Custom Agents is a Beginner lesson about automation, not about scaling knowledge.

questions.question: If a learner completes the "Essentials Path," which of the following skills are they most likely to have mastered?

options.text: Designing complex workspace architectures and automating processes with AI.
options.explanation: Those are Advanced Path topics, not covered in the Essentials Path.

options.text: Creating, collaborating on, and organizing work efficiently in Notion.
options.explanation: The Essentials Path is described as giving basic skills to create, collaborate, and organize work efficiently.

options.text: Building custom integrations that connect Notion with external SaaS tools.
options.explanation: Integrations are mentioned only in the "Custom Agents" lesson, not as a core outcome of the Essentials Path.

options.text: Managing Notion’s marketplace pricing and revenue models.
options.explanation: Marketplace pricing is not part of the Essentials learning objectives.

questions.question: Which of the following resources is NOT explicitly listed as a way to continue learning after exploring Notion Academy lessons?

options.explanation: Help Center is listed under "Still learning?" as a resource.
Live webinars are mentioned as a way to level up skills.
The article refers to "Join our community" but does not specifically name a forum; it emphasizes connecting with users, which is broader.
On‑demand sessions are part of the live webinar offering.

questions.question: How long is the "Templates & marketplace" lesson, and what difficulty level is it assigned?

options.explanation: The article lists the lesson as 40 min and marks it Intermediate.
The duration and difficulty do not match the listed details.
Those values belong to a different lesson.
The lesson length and level are incorrect.
